quizQuestion:
{___} con ella, pero no tengo tiempo.
(I would talk to her, but I don't have time.)
sentenceTranslation: I would talk to her, but I don't have time.
explanation: hablaría — simple conditional of hablar (yo). Formation: infinitive + endings: -ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -íais, -ían. All verbs use the same endings. Regular verbs add to full infinitive: hablar + ía = hablaría.

quizQuestion:
{___} más, pero estoy lleno.
(I would eat more, but I'm full.)
sentenceTranslation: I would eat more, but I'm full.
explanation: comería — simple conditional of comer (yo). comer + ía = comería. Note: -er/-ir verbs add endings directly to infinitive, just like -ar verbs. The endings always have an accent: -ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -íais, -ían.

quizQuestion:
{___} en España si tuviera dinero.
(I would live in Spain if I had money.)
sentenceTranslation: I would live in Spain if I had money.
explanation: viviría — simple conditional of vivir (yo). vivir + ía = viviría. This is a type 2 si-clause structure (B2+): si tuviera... + conditional. For now, note the conditional form viviría.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} con él si pudieras?
(Would you talk to him if you could?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you talk to him if you could?
explanation: hablarías — simple conditional of hablar (tú). hablar + ías = hablarías. Tú conditional ends in -ías (with accent). Useful for hypothetical questions: ¿Qué harías? ¿Adónde irías?

quizQuestion:
¿Qué {___} en mi lugar?
(What would you do in my place?)
sentenceTranslation: What would you do in my place?
explanation: haría — conditional of hacer (yo). hacer drops -ce and adds r: har- + ía = haría. Irregular conditional stems for hacer: har-. Full paradigm: haría, harías, haría, haríamos, haríais, harían.

quizQuestion:
Yo no {___} eso.
(I wouldn't do that.)
sentenceTranslation: I wouldn't do that.
grammarPointLabel: simple conditional irregular: hacer → yo: haría
explanation: haría — conditional of hacer (yo). Note: the question 'Yo no haría eso' literally says 'I (yo) wouldn't do that.' har- stem + ía. Same irregular stems for future: haré, harás... and conditional: haría, harías...

quizQuestion:
No {___} eso si fuera tú.
(I wouldn't say that if I were you.)
sentenceTranslation: I wouldn't say that if I were you.
explanation: diría — conditional of decir (yo). decir drops -ec and adds r: dir- + ía = diría. dir- stem: diría, dirías, diría, diríamos, diríais, dirían.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} tiempo para ayudarme?
(Would you have time to help me?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you have time to help me?
explanation: tendrías — conditional of tener (tú). tener inserts -d-: tendr- + ías = tendrías. Same stem pattern: tendría, tendrías, tendría, tendríamos, tendríais, tendrían. Also future: tendré, tendrás...

quizQuestion:
¿{___} hablar más despacio?
(Could you speak more slowly?)
sentenceTranslation: Could you speak more slowly?
grammarPointLabel: simple conditional irregular: poder → podrías (polite request)
explanation: podrías — conditional of poder (tú). poder drops -e-: podr- + ías = podrías. Used for polite requests in B1: ¿Podrías...? = Could you...? Very common in service interactions. Formal: ¿Podría usted...?

quizQuestion:
¿{___} ayudarme, por favor?
(Could you help me, please?)
sentenceTranslation: Could you help me, please?
explanation: podría — conditional of poder (usted/él/yo). podr- + ía = podría. ¿Podría...? (usted) is the most formal way to make a polite request. Same form as yo: Yo podría hacerlo (I could do it).

quizQuestion:
¿{___} a la fiesta?
(Would you come to the party?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you come to the party?
explanation: vendrías — conditional of venir (tú). venir inserts -d-: vendr- + ías = vendrías. Same stem in future: vendré. Full paradigm: vendría, vendrías, vendría, vendríamos, vendríais, vendrían.

quizQuestion:
{___} descansar más.
(You should rest more.)
sentenceTranslation: You should rest more.
explanation: deberías — conditional of deber (tú), used for giving advice. Deberías + infinitive = you should... This is the standard B1 construction for advice: Deberías comer menos. Deberías estudiar más. (Not: Debes, which sounds like an order.)

quizQuestion:
Mañana {___} con mi jefe.
(Tomorrow I will talk to my boss.)
sentenceTranslation: Tomorrow I will talk to my boss.
explanation: hablaré — future simple of hablar (yo). Formation: infinitive + endings: -é, -ás, -á, -emos, -éis, -án. Regular verbs attach endings to the full infinitive: hablar + é = hablaré.

quizQuestion:
{___} juntos el domingo.
(We will eat together on Sunday.)
sentenceTranslation: We will eat together on Sunday.
explanation: comeremos — future simple of comer (nosotros). comer + emos = comeremos. Note: nosotros future ends in -emos (same as present for -er verbs but with accent on e in future: comerEmos vs. comEmos present).

quizQuestion:
{___} en esta ciudad para siempre.
(I will live in this city forever.)
sentenceTranslation: I will live in this city forever.
explanation: viviré — future simple of vivir (yo). vivir + é = viviré. -ir verbs: same pattern, attach to full infinitive. viviré, vivirás, vivirá, viviremos, viviréis, vivirán.

quizQuestion:
{___} los deberes esta tarde.
(I will do my homework this afternoon.)
sentenceTranslation: I will do my homework this afternoon.
explanation: haré — future of hacer (yo). Irregular stem har- + é = haré. Same stem as conditional: haría. Key memory link: if the conditional is haría, the future is haré (same stem, different endings).

quizQuestion:
¿Qué {___} este fin de semana?
(What will you do this weekend?)
sentenceTranslation: What will you do this weekend?
explanation: harás — future of hacer (tú). har- + ás = harás. Full paradigm: haré, harás, hará, haremos, haréis, harán. The classic question ¿Qué harás? tests both the irregular stem and future tense usage.

quizQuestion:
Le {___} la verdad mañana.
(I will tell him the truth tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: I will tell him the truth tomorrow.
explanation: diré — future of decir (yo). dir- + é = diré. decir and hacer both drop their infinitive ending and contract: dir-, har-. diré, dirás, dirá, diremos, diréis, dirán.

quizQuestion:
{___} que trabajar el sábado.
(I will have to work on Saturday.)
sentenceTranslation: I will have to work on Saturday.
explanation: tendré — future of tener (yo). tener inserts -d-: tendr- + é = tendré. Same stem group: tener → tendr-, venir → vendr-, salir → saldr-, poner → pondr-. tendré que + infinitive = will have to...

quizQuestion:
¿{___} venir mañana?
(Will you be able to come tomorrow?)
sentenceTranslation: Will you be able to come tomorrow?
explanation: podrás — future of poder (tú). poder drops -e-: podr- + ás = podrás. Common question structure: ¿Podrás...? (future ability). Contrast with ¿Podrías...? (conditional, polite request).

quizQuestion:
¿{___} a la reunión?
(Will you come to the meeting?)
sentenceTranslation: Will you come to the meeting?
explanation: vendrás — future of venir (tú). vendr- + ás = vendrás. vendr- stem: vendré, vendrás, vendrá, vendremos, vendréis, vendrán. Don't confuse with conditional: vendría (same stem, -ía endings).

quizQuestion:
Si estudias, {___} el examen.
(If you study, you will pass the exam.)
sentenceTranslation: If you study, you will pass the exam.
explanation: aprobarás — future of aprobar in a type 1 si-clause. Pattern: si + present indicative + future simple. The si clause uses present indicative (not subjunctive). Result clause uses future. Si estudias → you study (condition), aprobarás → you will pass (result).

quizQuestion:
Si no llueve, {___} al parque.
(If it doesn't rain, we'll go to the park.)
sentenceTranslation: If it doesn't rain, we'll go to the park.
explanation: iremos — future of ir (nosotros). Si clause: si no llueve (present indicative). Result: iremos (future). Si-clauses type 1 express real/likely conditions. Contrast type 2 (B2+): si no lloviera, iríamos (imperfect subj + conditional).
